name: ChatTTS
tagline: Generative text-to-speech model built for natural daily dialogue
metaDescription: ChatTTS is an open-source generative text-to-speech model for dialogue, with multi-speaker voices and fine-grained control over prosody, laughter, and pauses.
overview: ChatTTS is a generative text-to-speech model designed for dialogue scenarios such as LLM assistants. Instead of reading text in a flat, robotic way, it aims to produce speech that sounds natural and expressive in a back-and-forth conversation.

The model supports English and Chinese, and can render multiple speakers so interactive conversations feel more lifelike. The open-source release on Hugging Face is a 40,000-hour pre-trained base model, intended for academic and research use under a non-commercial license.
features: Conversational TTS optimized for dialogue, with support for multiple speakers in interactive conversations
Fine-grained control over prosody, including laughter, pauses, and interjections via in-text tokens
Better prosody than most open-source TTS models, with pretrained weights provided for further research
Sentence-level and word-level control using tokens like [oral_2], [laugh], [uv_break], and [break_6]
Streaming audio generation and zero-shot inference with the open-sourced DVAE encoder
Sample random speaker embeddings from a Gaussian and save them to recover the same timbre later

gettingStarted.intro: ChatTTS is distributed as a Python package on PyPI. Install it, load the model, and call infer() with your text to produce audio. The example below mirrors the basic usage in the project README.

steps.heading: Install from PyPI
steps.body: Install the stable version of the ChatTTS package with pip.

steps.heading: Generate speech in Python
steps.body: Load the model and run inference on a list of texts, then save each result as a WAV file at 24000 Hz.
steps.code:
import ChatTTS
import torch
import torchaudio

chat = ChatTTS.Chat()
chat.load(compile=False) # Set to True for better performance

texts = ["PUT YOUR 1st TEXT HERE", "PUT YOUR 2nd TEXT HERE"]

wavs = chat.infer(texts)

for i in range(len(wavs)):
    torchaudio.save(f"basic_output{i}.wav", torch.from_numpy(wavs[i]).unsqueeze(0), 24000)

steps.heading: Try the command line or WebUI
steps.body: From the project root you can also infer directly from the command line, which saves audio to ./output_audio_n.mp3, or launch the bundled web interface.
steps.code:
python examples/cmd/run.py "Your text 1." "Your text 2."
python examples/web/webui.py

useCases: Giving an LLM assistant or chatbot a natural, expressive spoken voice for dialogue
Research and academic experiments on prosody and conversational speech synthesis
Generating multi-speaker English and Chinese audio with controllable laughter, pauses, and intonation
